pages.title: Dental Check-Ups & Oral Cancer Screening
pages.metaDescription: Routine dental exams with calm monitoring, preventative advice, and reassuring screening.

contentBlocks.label: Overview
contentBlocks.title: Gentle check-ups with attentive screening
contentBlocks.summary: We keep routine exams unhurried, explaining what we see and what it means for your mouth. We include a visual oral cancer screening at every routine exam.

contentBlocks.label: Frequency
contentBlocks.title: How often to book
contentBlocks.summary: Most people stay on track with 6-monthly visits. If you have higher decay or gum risk, recent treatment, or are in active orthodontic care, we may agree a shorter cadence. We explain any recommendations so you know why timing matters.

contentBlocks.label: Support
contentBlocks.title: If something is worrying you
contentBlocks.summary: If you notice ulcers that do not settle, new lumps, persistent soreness, or changes to your mouth or throat, contact us promptly. We will arrange a calm assessment, share what we can see, and signpost to further investigation if needed.

contentBlocks.title: Stay on track between check-ups
contentBlocks.summary: Keep hygiene, preventative care, and family visits on a steady rhythm.

contentBlocks.title: Arrange your routine dental check-up
contentBlocks.summary: Book a calm examination with oral cancer screening included. Tell us if you would like a slower pace or extra reassurance.

pages.title: Composite bonding

contentBlocks.title: Composite bonding for measured smile refinement
contentBlocks.summary: Composite bonding (also called cosmetic bonding or dental bonding) uses layered resin to rebuild edges and balance shapes with minimal drilling. We preserve enamel wherever possible, plan conservatively, and explain how wear and staining can be managed over time.

contentBlocks.label: Introduction
contentBlocks.title: Conservative improvements, well planned
contentBlocks.summary: Composite bonding uses tooth-coloured resin to improve shape, close small gaps, repair chips, or soften uneven edges. It can often be done with minimal drilling, making it a conservative option when the bite and enamel are suitable. We begin with an assessment of wear, staining,…

contentBlocks.title: What composite bonding can and can’t do

contentBlocks.label: Digital planning
contentBlocks.title: Digitally planned and guided bonding

contentBlocks.title: Severe toothache and dental pain
contentBlocks.summary: Severe toothache can feel urgent, and it’s hard to think about anything else. Here in Shoreham-by-Sea, we see how quickly pain can disrupt sleep, eating, and work. Pain relief can help you cope, but it doesn’t fix the underlying cause. Toothache can come from decay, infection, c…

Toothache can range from mild twinges to severe, throbbing pain that disrupts sleep. If it’s persistent, worsening, or triggered by biting, heat, or cold, it’s worth getting checked even if it comes and goes. We can talk through what’s happening and advise timing. If pain is get…

contentBlocks.label: Prompt relief

contentBlocks.summary: Pain often means the tooth or surrounding tissues are under stress. Painkillers and temporary measures can take the edge off, but they cannot resolve infection, nerve damage, or structural problems such as cracks or failed restorations. The safest route is a proper assessment so…

contentBlocks.summary: Tooth wear refers to the gradual loss of tooth structure over time. It’s a common issue and often develops slowly, meaning many people don’t notice it until changes in shape, sensitivity, or appearance become more obvious. At St Mary’s House Dental Care, tooth wear is managed th…

contentBlocks.label: Assessment
contentBlocks.title: Why careful assessment matters
contentBlocks.summary: Understanding the cause and pattern of wear helps guide appropriate management. Assessment may include examining how teeth meet, looking for signs of muscle activity, reviewing diet and habits, and checking for changes over time. Not all wear requires immediate treatment, but mo…

contentBlocks.title: When treatment may be needed
contentBlocks.summary: If tooth wear is advanced or causing functional or comfort issues, treatment may be discussed. Options can range from simple protective measures to restorative approaches designed to rebuild worn surfaces. Any proposed treatment is planned carefully and staged where possible.

contentBlocks.label: Long-term outlook
contentBlocks.title: Long-term outlook
contentBlocks.summary: Tooth wear is usually a long-term consideration rather than a quick fix. Ongoing review helps ensure that teeth remain comfortable and functional, and that any intervention remains appropriate as circumstances change.

contentBlocks.title: Restoring worn or damaged teeth
contentBlocks.summary: Compare protective and restorative steps before deciding what to do next.

contentBlocks.title: Plan a calm review for tooth wear or broken edges
contentBlocks.summary: Contact us to discuss concerns about tooth wear and appropriate next steps.

pages.title: Sensitive teeth & whitening

contentBlocks.title: Whitening with sensitivity in mind
contentBlocks.summary: We plan whitening for sensitive teeth using gentle gel strengths, protective trays, and pacing. Every step is monitored by your dentist so comfort leads the plan.

contentBlocks.title: Designed to be tolerable for sensitive teeth
Sensitivity-aware whitening options
contentBlocks.summary: If sensitivity is the main issue, it can help to address the cause first — such as gum health, tooth wear, or hygiene care — then decide whether whitening still makes sense.

contentBlocks.title: Plan whitening that respects sensitivity
contentBlocks.summary: Book a review so we can tailor gel strength, pacing, and aftercare to your comfort.

pages.title: Whitening top-ups & maintenance

contentBlocks.title: Keep your whitening results steady
contentBlocks.summary: Dentist-guided top-ups reuse your existing trays and follow a conservative schedule. We protect enamel, match refreshes to hygiene visits, and avoid over-whitening.

contentBlocks.title: A quick check before any refresh
contentBlocks.summary: We review your shade, tray fit, and any recent dental work before recommending more gel. This keeps maintenance aligned with your oral health and any planned bonding or veneer updates.

contentBlocks.title: Refresh without starting from scratch
contentBlocks.summary: Top-ups are calibrated to your lifestyle so results stay natural, not artificial.

contentBlocks.title: Keeping whitening results steady
contentBlocks.summary: Compare top-ups, sensitivity support, and routine reviews before the next visit.

contentBlocks.title: Schedule a maintenance review
contentBlocks.summary: Refresh your whitening plan with dentist guidance and the trays you already have.
